title: The Plus-que-parfait
description: Talk about an action that happened before another past action, using the imparfait of avoir/être plus the past participle.

cards.back: how to build the plus-que-parfait (had done)
cards.notes: *j'avais mangé* (I had eaten), *elle était partie* (she had left).

cards.front: Quand je suis arrivé, il était déjà parti.
cards.back: When I arrived, he had already left.
cards.notes: The plus-que-parfait marks the **earlier** of two past actions.

cards.front: être verbs: aller, venir, partir, arriver, rester…
cards.back: verbs of movement/state use être (and agree)
cards.notes: Same être-verbs as the passé composé; the participle agrees with the subject.
Often appears with the plus-que-parfait: *il était déjà parti*.

cards.front: Il a dit qu'il avait fini.
cards.back: He said that he had finished.
cards.notes: In reported speech, a past-of-a-past becomes the plus-que-parfait.

steps.title: Forming the plus-que-parfait
steps.body:
# The plus-que-parfait (the past-before-the-past)

It works just like the passé composé, but the auxiliary is in the **imparfait**:

> **avais / était** (imparfait of avoir or être) + **participe passé**

| Auxiliary | Example | English |
|---|---|---|
| avoir | j'**avais mangé** | I had eaten |
| être | elle **était partie** | she had left |

The choice of auxiliary and the agreement rules are the **same** as the passé composé:

- avoir for most verbs (no agreement with the subject)
- être for movement/state verbs — and the participle **agrees**: *elle était parti**e***, *ils étaient parti**s***.

steps.title: When to use it
steps.body:
# Using the plus-que-parfait

It marks the **earlier** of two past events — what had *already* happened:

- *Quand je suis arrivé, il **était** déjà **parti**.* — When I arrived, he had already left.
- *Nous **avions fini** avant qu'ils n'arrivent.* — We had finished before they arrived.

It also appears in **reported speech**, for an action already past at the time of speaking:

- *Il a dit qu'il **avait fini**.* — He said that he had finished.

The adverb **déjà** (already) is a common companion.

exercise.prompt: Match each French plus-que-parfait form with its English meaning.
Fill in the imparfait of avoir: "I had eaten before the film."
exercise.hint: manger uses avoir; imparfait je-form: avais.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the imparfait of être: "When I arrived, he had already left."
exercise.sentence: Quand je suis arrivé, il ___ déjà parti.
exercise.hint: partir uses être; imparfait il-form: était.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *She had already left.* (partir → être, feminine agreement)
exercise.hint: être + partie (feminine agreement) + déjà between auxiliary and participle.

exercise.prompt: Arrange: *He said that he had finished.*
exercise.hint: passé composé (a dit) + que + plus-que-parfait (avait fini).
